content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 31,
1992
Geneva Brett
Committee To Elect Ken Duran
730 San Benito Street
Hollister,
CA
95023
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-92-441
Dear Ms. Brett:
This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice concerning the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1
On May 21,
1992,
the Committee to Elect Ken Duran made an
expenditure in the amount of $1,700 for the preparation of a
mailer in support of the committee’s controlling candidate,
Ken
Duran.
The expenditure was made during the 16 days immediately
preceding the election in which the candidate was being voted on.
This letter will confirm my advice to you that an expenditure made
out of campaign funds by a candidate’s controlled committee on
behalf of the candidate is not required to be reported within 24
hours of making the expenditure.
The expenditure must be reported
on the next regular campaign report that the committee would be
required to file.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
42S J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 80?
•
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660


Geneva Brett
A--92—441
Page
2
If you have further questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Assistant Chief Counsel
By:
Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant

sections.conclusion: This letter will confirm my advice to you that an expenditure made out of campaign funds by a candidate's controlled committee on behalf of the candidate is not required to be reported within 24 hours of making the expenditure. The expenditure must be reported on the next regular campaign report that the committee would be required to file.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate's controlled committee must report within 24 hours an expenditure made out of campaign funds during the 16 days immediately preceding an election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that campaign expenditures made by a candidate's controlled committee during the 16-day period preceding an election are not required to be reported within 24 hours, but instead must be reported on the next regular campaign report filing.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter for prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present, but question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable in the letter body. Document references Government Code Sections 81000-91015 and relates to the Political Reform Act.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a candidate's controlled committee must report within 24 hours an expenditure made out of campaign funds during the 16 days immediately preceding an election.

CONCLUSION: This letter will confirm my advice to you that an expenditure made out of campaign funds by a candidate's controlled committee on behalf of the candidate is not required to be reported within 24 hours of making the expenditure. The expenditure must be reported on the next regular campaign report that the committee would be required to file.

The expenditure was made during the 16 days immediately preceding the election in which the candidate was being voted on. This letter will confirm my advice to you that an expenditure made out of campaign funds by a candidate’s controlled committee on behalf of the candidate is not required to be reported within 24 hours of making the expenditure. The expenditure must be reported on the next regular campaign report that the committee would be required to file. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 42S J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Assistant Chief Counsel By: Sandra L. Taylor Political Reform Consultant
embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding campaign expenditure reporting requirements for the Committee to Elect Ken Duran, clarifying that a $1,700 pre-election expenditure need not be reported within 24 hours but rather on the next regular campaign report.
